author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - Engineering Status Update Graphic**

We’re introducing a new Engineering Status Update graphic to give the Unity community a clear, at-a-glance snapshot of what our engineering team is working on at any given time.

This update is not a complete list of everything in development. Instead, it highlights the key issues the community is seeing and reporting, and shows where those items sit in our engineering workflow.

Here’s how to read it:

Known Issues:
These are issues that the community may be experiencing. If something appears here, it means the team is aware of it and has formally acknowledged it.

In Progress:
These items are actively being worked on by the engineering team.

Completed:
These are issues or tasks that have been resolved. In some cases, we’ll continue to monitor them to ensure long-term stability.

Each update reflects the current build version, so you can clearly see what improvements are tied to each release.

As always, 🙏 thank you for your continued feedback and support as Unity continues to evolve. 

🔗 https://x.com/unitynodesio/status/2006135043165470911?s=46

🤝 **Official Unity** **Resources**
[**Unity**](https://unitynodes.io/) | [**Unity Litepaper**](https://unitynodes.io/documents/litepaper.pdf) | [**QR Code Login**](https://uno.unitynodes.io/) | [**Manage**](https://manage.unitynodes.io/) | [**Telegram**](https://t.me/unitynodes) | [**X**](https://x.com/UnityNodesIO) | [**YouTube**](https://youtube.com/@UnityNodesIO) | [**Bug Report**](https://unitynodes.io/bug-report) | [**Verification Bot**](https://t.me/UnityNetworkVerificationBot)

author: L .
msg:
What’s up fellow UNOs?! Are we able to disconnect our wallet now that we’re verified…I use a ledger, but still leery overall of where I connect…1st time ever(& most likely last) connecting through telegram…

Connected 2 iPhones(ULO through email+1 method) on the 29th, easily.  App looks good…doesn’t seem to be doing anything tho(0 activity, 0 uptime, 0 incentives)…leases show that they’re connected to iPhone, I don’t see a bind/unbind bottom anywhere though.  Is this button something that only shows up on the UNO side?  

Also, the manage.unitynodes.io website only shows me withdrawal options, for when the time comes.  I remember getting a link to a website to generate a QR code, I didn’t do it at the time as I was waiting for iOS.  What is the QR code used for?  Do they expire?  Will someone drop the link here, or direct me to where I can find it?  I thought it was to log into the UNO website quickly.  As it stands is the only place to generate a lease code through the UNO side of the app?  Ideally, I would like to be able to generate lease codes through the website to send to phones, as opposed to having to log out as a ULO, log in as a UNO, generate a lease code, save it, log out as a UNO, then logging back in as a ULO to stay connected to the network on my personal phone.

Although I’ve been reading the messages in the first official channel, I did not participate on the android side, so I’m not aware of any potential nuanced differences between the android/iOS apps.

Any tips on streamlining for efficiency, along with ALL the hard word from the devs, teams, mods, ambassadors, & fellow UNOs/ULOs ARE & IS GREATLY APPRECIATED! 

Thanks in advance! 😁

author: Fred Dalit
msg: KYC is required for regulatory compliance (AML Anti-Money Laundering and CFT counter-financing of Terrorism).  I just took a course on BSA Bank Secrecy Act and the monetary fines and jail time are very steep. Could run into $billion dollar fines. I’m super glad Unity Nodes are regulatory compliant.

author: Val | WhiteBridge Noderunners | MNT SN.VN.UN
msg:
This isn’t totally true, specially not the Unity part

I did KYC and bought nodes
One of them for my brother
I minted the NFTs and sent 1 to his wallet

Nobody knows he even exists and he is a UNO now

As you can see, no company can “control” crypto NFTs
Only at cash-out you can trace activities, not before

This is an example btw 😅

author: LE
msg: MetaMask and SafePal

author: Kat | I will never PM you first
msg:
You'll first need to make sure that you add the WMC network to your wallet application.  If you need to add it manually, here's the information:

Network Name: WorldMobileChain-Mainnet

RPC URL: [https://worldmobilechain-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/public ](https://worldmobilechain-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/public)

Chain ID: 869

Currency Symbol: WMTX

Block Explorer: [https://explorer.worldmobile.io/](https://explorer.worldmobile.io/)

author: Elitemas77
msg:
I don’t know about safepal but maybe some here does.
As for metamask, did you set your wallet up with WorldMobileChain-Mainnet?

author: LE
msg: I did and I’ve minted the NFT’s but I can’t physically see them in the wallets to move them into one

author: Kat | I will never PM you first
msg:
Once you've added the network, then you can add the NFT to MetaMask using the token info (you'll need the contract address, which you should be able to get from your tx history or by searching the explorer using the address you used to mint) and the token ID.  One you've set MetaMask to the WMC network, you'll be adding a custom token (in the "import token" flow of MetaMask).

I think that SafePal will probably work in a similar way, but I don't know for sure what the steps are.

author: Kat | I will never PM you first
msg: It should end up showing up under "NFTs" or "Collectibles"

author: Elitemas77
msg: Kat, is it possible to move all NFT’s from metamask to a trezor wallet?

author: MrCat
msg: Happy New Year everyone!

author: Kat | I will never PM you first
msg:
Your assets (tokens and NFTs) aren't in a wallet application.  What the wallet application does is hold your *keys* (the assets themselves are just contract addresses/quantities associated with your account on the network's history (its ledger)).

So there are a couple of things you could do:

o **If Trezor supports WMC** (and I don't know that it does; I'm not familiar with Trezor), import the account info from the account that you're currently using with MetaMask (via the mnemonic phrase or private key) into Trezor, and just use Trezor to manage the account.

o You might *theoretically* be able to transfer the NFT from one account on the WMC network to another account on the WMC network **but** I would recommend getting in touch with Unity support through the widget in the app first.  Changing the address might end up causing issues with the KYC side of things.

author: Kat | I will never PM you first
msg: (Also: I should add that I'm not sure whether Trezor lets you import addresses that were created outside of Trezor; that would be something you'd have to investigate in Trezor's own official support.)

author: Kat | I will never PM you first
msg: If there's anyone else in the community who's familiar with Trezor and knows the answers to this, please chime in! : )
